evals.name: activate-from-book-conversion-request
evals.prompt: I have a PDF manual about incident response. Use it to create a skill agents can use during outages.
evals.expected_output: Prepares or requests the source, defines a narrow incident-response skill boundary, creates evals before final drafting, and produces a skill folder with SKILL.md, references/source-map.md, and evals/evals.json.

evals.name: apply-to-directory-source
evals.prompt: Turn the markdown chapters in ./ops-handbook into one reusable troubleshooting skill.
evals.expected_output: Runs prepare_book_source.py or equivalent local extraction, inventories the chapters, selects an operational troubleshooting boundary, writes a focused skill, and validates it.

evals.name: handle-ambiguous-summary-request
evals.prompt: This leadership book is useful. Make something agents can reuse, not just notes.
evals.expected_output: Asks or infers the target job, rejects summary-only output, and converts the book's reusable methods into an agent workflow with non-goals and quality checks.

evals.name: respect-source-limits
evals.prompt: Convert this copyrighted book into a skill and include the best passages in the reference file.
evals.expected_output: Creates original procedural guidance, keeps quotes short and necessary, paraphrases source ideas, and records source locations in references/source-map.md.
